sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD has struggled recently, managing just one win in their last five matches, with a notable 3-0 victory against Sevilla but losses and a draw otherwise. Espanyol, on the other hand, shows better form with three wins in their last five, including back-to-back victories against Athletic Club and Getafe, though they suffered a 2-0 loss to Barcelona. This suggests Espanyol has the momentum heading into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Espanyol has dominated Levante UD, winning both matches (2-1 and 4-1). Historically, Espanyol has a strong record with no losses in the last four meetings, and recent games have seen high-scoring outcomes with over 2.5 goals in four of the last five clashes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Levante UD sits 19th in the league with 13 points, struggling defensively with 29 goals conceded and low possession stats (41.3%, 19th in the league). Espanyol, positioned 5th with 33 points, boasts a balanced attack and defense, conceding only 19 goals while scoring 22, indicating a more cohesive unit compared to Levante’s relegation battle.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Levante, Etta Eyong has been a bright spot with 6 goals this season, while Espanyol’s Pere Milla also has 6 goals and could exploit Levante’s leaky defense. Espanyol’s Edu Expósito, with 5 assists and a high Sofascore rating, will be crucial in creating chances.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Levante’s low possession and high clearance stats suggest a defensive, counter-attacking approach, but their tendency to concede first (7/9 games) could be exploited by Espanyol’s ability to score early (often first to score in head-to-heads). Espanyol’s stronger ground duels and recent form indicate they might control the midfield battle.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Espanyol’s superior form, league standing, and head-to-head advantage, they are favorites to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 54% away win probability. Levante’s home struggles and defensive frailties make an upset unlikely, though they might score given both teams’ tendency to concede in these matchups.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline between Levante UD and Espanyol remains level with no goals scored by either side. Levante UD has enjoyed a slight edge in ball possession, controlling the game for a majority of the first half. However, Espanyol has looked more threatening in attack, creating a couple of significant opportunities but failing to convert them. Both teams have shown moments of promise in the final third, yet neither has managed to break the deadlock in a tightly contested match so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Levante UD
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD has shown a decent level of control in the game, with more time on the ball compared to their opponents. Their entries into the final third have been frequent, but they’ve struggled to translate this into clear-cut chances. The team has been somewhat cautious in attack, with limited shots on target. Defensively, they’ve held firm, managing to neutralize most of Espanyol’s threats. Key players like Iván Romero and Adrián de la Fuente have contributed with composure and defensive solidity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Espanyol
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol has been the more dangerous side in terms of attacking intent, despite having less possession. They’ve created a couple of big chances but were unable to capitalize, showing a lack of clinical finishing. Their presence in the final third matches Levante’s, and they’ve won a higher percentage of duels, indicating physical dominance. Players like Edu Expósito and Carlos Romero have been pivotal, with Expósito coming close to scoring. Espanyol’s defensive structure has also been robust, limiting Levante’s opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: In the opening minutes of the game, Levante UD dominated the attacking momentum, pushing forward with consistent spells of pressure. As the half progressed, there were noticeable shifts, with Espanyol gradually gaining traction and creating sustained attacking phases around the midway point. Levante regained some initiative with intermittent bursts of forward play, but towards the closing stages of the half, Espanyol mounted a significant surge, ending the period as the more aggressive side in attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle, with both teams likely to push for a breakthrough. Espanyol appears to have the upper hand based on their first-half chances and higher expected goals, suggesting they could capitalize if they maintain their attacking intent. Levante UD, on the other hand, will look to make better use of their possession and create more dangerous opportunities in the final third. Tactical adjustments could play a crucial role, especially for Levante, who need to be more incisive in attack. Espanyol’s physicality and ability to win duels may give them an edge if the game opens up.
